channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Hezbollah leader rejects Lebanon-Israel direct talks, vows to confront Israel
item.description: Hezbollah leader has rejected direct talks with Israel, vowing to confront them. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi arrived in Saint Petersburg for talks with Vladimir Putin on war developments. Meeting with Vladimir Putin will be a good opportunity to discuss war developments, says Iran FM Araghchi; Iran FM arrives in Saint Petersburg for Putin talks, says Iranian state media.

item.title: Worker killed at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant after drone strike, says Russia
item.description: A worker was killed at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power station after a Ukrainian drone attack, according to Russian forces. The plant is under Russian control. A worker at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power ​station has been killed in a ​Ukrainian drone attack, according to Russian ​forces, which control the plant.

item.title: Iran’s foreign minister in Russia for talks
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister arrived in Russia for talks. No details on the agenda were provided. Iran’s foreign minister in Russia for talks.

item.title: ASEAN ministers pledge to safeguard regional energy security amid Middle East tensions
item.description: ASEAN ministers agreed to safeguard regional energy security through cooperation and policy responses. The move is amid rising tensions in the Middle East. The regional bloc agreed to safeguard regional energy security through strengthened cooperation, timely policy responses and engagement with dialogue partners.

item.title: Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally meets with pope and prays at the Vatican
item.description: Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally met with the Pope and prayed at the Vatican. She is on a four-day pilgrimage to Rome, visiting pontifical basilicas and meeting with Vatican officials.

item.title: Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights
item.description: Ukraine's drone commander, Robert Brovdi, shared in a rare interview that his unit destroys a third of all targets on the battlefield. His unit targets Russian oil, troops, and morale.

item.title: Beijing blocks Meta's acquisition of Chinese AI startup Manus
item.description: Beijing blocked Meta's acquisition of Chinese AI startup Manus. Meta acquired Manus in December 2025 for over $2 billion to boost AI agent capabilities. Meta, which owns Facebook, acquired Manus in December 2025 for more than US$2 billion in a bid to boost its capabilities in AI agents.

item.title: Will the Iran war end supremacy of Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: The Iran conflict exposed the energy market's vulnerability, specifically the Strait of Hormuz. Global powers are pushing renewables while Gulf leaders plan new bypass pipelines. The conflict has brutally exposed the energy market's Achilles heel.

item.title: India, New Zealand Ink 'Once-In-A-Generation' Pact: All You Need To Know
item.description: India and New Zealand signed a free trade agreement, granting Indian exporters 100 percent duty-free access to New Zealand. The pact is considered a once-in-a-generation opportunity. India-New Zealand FTA: Indian exporters will get 100 per centduty-free access to New Zealand.

item.title: Musk vs. OpenAI trial to get underway
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is taking Sam Altmann's OpenAI to court, accusing Altmann of betraying OpenAI's original nonprofit mission. The trial marks a courtroom battle between two AI sector giants. Two giants in the AI sector are set for a courtroom battle: Elon Musk (xAI) is taking on Sam Altmann (OpenAI).

item.title: Mali defence minister killed amid flurry of insurgent attacks
item.description: Sadio Camara, Mali's defence minister, was killed in a car bomb attack at his residence, the government said. The attack occurred during coordinated assaults by insurgent groups, including the West African affiliate of al-Qaida.

item.title: Iran proposes to reopen Strait of Hormuz without nuclear agreement
item.description: Iran's foreign minister proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz without a nuclear agreement. He blamed the US for the failure of Pakistan talks, according to state media. Iran FM in Saint Petersburg blames U. S.

item.title: Michael Jackson biopic smashes box office record
item.description: The Michael Jackson biopic broke a box office record, earning $217m globally despite receiving poor reviews. The film's success has been notable despite its critical reception. The film received poor reviews but has stormed the box office, taking $217m (£160m) globally.

item.title: Authorities investigating cybersecurity incident at firm involved in MRT and NEWater projects
item.description: Authorities are investigating a cybersecurity incident at Shanghai Tunnel Engineering Co, a firm involved in Singapore's MRT and NEWater projects. The incident did not affect the construction of the Jurong Region Line or compromise sensitive data.

item.title: Iran's 'Quadruple' Warning To Gulf Nations After Trump's Threats
item.description: Iran responded to US President Trump's threats, highlighting its advantages in the standoff. Iranian official Ghalibaf made the comments. Ghalibaf also responded to Trump's threat, highlighting Iran's advantage in the standoff.

item.title: Suspected gunman at Washington press dinner identified as 31-year-old Californian
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old Californian, was identified as the suspected gunman arrested at a Washington press dinner attended by President Trump. The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump has been named in US media as Cole Tomas Allen from California.

item.title: Torrential rain in southern China leads to flooding fears
item.description: Heavy rain is sweeping over southern China, with rainfall totals expected to exceed 100mm across many parts of six provinces by Wednesday. Some areas may see as much as 150-200mm, prompting flooding fears. The Office of the State Flood Control and Drought Relief is on alert.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In
item.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif that the US should remove operational obstacles, including the blockade, to revive talks. The talks collapsed with Iran blaming the US for the failure. Pezeshkian's minister arrived for talks.

item.title: At least 42 killed in Chad after water well dispute escalates
item.description: A water well dispute between two families in Chad escalated into reprisal attacks, killing at least 42 people. The cycle of violence has not been contained. Authorities have not commented on the incident.

item.title: Iran offers to end chokehold on Strait of Hormuz, asks U.S. to end blockade
item.description: Iran offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz if the US ends its blockade of the country. The proposal was made through closed-door negotiations, with two officials discussing the terms. The US has not publicly responded.
